reported.definition: Expectation is fully addressed with documented evidence of implementation, quantified metrics where applicable, and integration into institutional processes

partial.definition: Expectation is partially addressed — some elements implemented but coverage is incomplete, limited to qualitative statements, or restricted to a subset of risk categories or business lines

missing.definition: Expectation is not addressed or no evidence found in disclosures, risk management documentation, or supervisory reports

N/A.definition: Expectation is not applicable to this entity (e.g. specific banking activities not conducted by the institution)

critical.condition: Score 0-1 AND high materiality for the expectation
critical.action: Immediate remediation required — likely to result in supervisory findings in SREP or thematic review
examples: No climate risk integration in risk management framework (E7)
No consideration of climate risks in credit granting (E8)
Climate risks absent from risk appetite framework (E4)

high.condition: Score 0-1 AND medium materiality for the expectation
high.action: Priority remediation — significant supervisory risk and potential Pillar 2 implications
examples: No organisational assignment of climate risk responsibilities (E5)
No internal reporting on climate risk exposures (E6)
No assessment of climate impacts on liquidity (E11)
No business environment monitoring for climate risks (E2)

medium.action: Planned improvement — initial steps taken but not yet meeting ECB expectations
examples: Climate risks considered only for high-carbon sectors in credit process
Qualitative climate disclosures without quantitative metrics
Initial climate risk appetite indicators without operational limits

low.action: Continuous improvement — meets minimum expectations, opportunity to align with leading practices
examples: Enhance stress testing with additional climate scenarios
Extend counterparty-level climate risk scoring to full portfolio
Add forward-looking climate metrics to disclosures
Integrate climate factors into advanced internal models

no_climate_risk_framework.title: Climate Risks Not Integrated in Risk Management Framework
no_climate_risk_framework.description: Institution has not incorporated climate-related and environmental risks as drivers of existing prudential risk categories. This is a core ECB expectation (E7) and a foundational gap for supervisory compliance.
no_climate_risk_framework.recommendation: Map climate and environmental risks to all prudential risk categories (credit, market, operational, liquidity). Document risk identification methodology and integrate into risk taxonomy.

no_credit_risk_integration.title: Climate Risks Not Considered in Credit Granting Process
no_credit_risk_integration.description: Institution does not consider climate-related risks at any stage of the credit-granting process (E8). ECB expects climate risk assessment in origination, monitoring, and review.
no_credit_risk_integration.recommendation: Integrate climate risk assessment into credit origination policies, develop sector-level risk classifications, and establish portfolio monitoring for climate-sensitive exposures.

no_climate_disclosures.title: No Climate-Related Risk Disclosures
no_climate_disclosures.description: Institution does not publish meaningful information on climate-related and environmental risks (E12/E13). Supervisory expectations require structured disclosures aligned with regulatory frameworks.
no_climate_disclosures.recommendation: Develop disclosure policy covering governance, strategy, risk management and metrics. Align with Pillar 3 ESG requirements and TCFD framework.

no_risk_appetite_climate.title: Climate Risks Absent from Risk Appetite Framework
no_risk_appetite_climate.description: Institution has not explicitly included climate-related and environmental risks in its risk appetite framework (E4). Without climate-related limits and indicators, risk-taking is not bounded.
no_risk_appetite_climate.recommendation: Define climate-related risk appetite indicators and limits (e.g. sector concentration limits for high-carbon exposures, green asset ratio targets). Integrate into ICAAP.

no_governance_assignment.title: No Assignment of Climate Risk Responsibilities
no_governance_assignment.description: Institution has not assigned responsibility for climate-related and environmental risk management within its organisational structure (E5). Three lines of defence model not applied to C&E risks.
no_governance_assignment.recommendation: Assign dedicated climate risk responsibilities across all three lines of defence. Establish board-level oversight and ensure adequate resources and expertise.

no_internal_reporting.title: No Internal Reporting on Climate Risk Exposures
no_internal_reporting.description: Institution does not produce internal reports with aggregated risk data on climate-related exposures (E6). Management body cannot make informed decisions on C&E risk management.
no_internal_reporting.recommendation: Establish regular internal reporting with aggregated climate risk data including sectoral concentrations, physical risk exposure maps and transition risk indicators.

no_climate_stress_testing.title: No Climate Stress Testing or Scenario Analysis
no_climate_stress_testing.description: Institution has not developed stress-testing scenarios incorporating climate-related and environmental risks (E10). ECB expects climate scenarios in market risk and broader stress testing.
no_climate_stress_testing.recommendation: Develop and implement climate stress-testing scenarios covering both transition and physical risk pathways. Integrate results into risk management and capital planning.

limited_disclosure_metrics.title: Limited Climate Risk Metrics Disclosed
limited_disclosure_metrics.description: Institution discloses some qualitative information but lacks quantitative key metrics on climate risk vulnerabilities (E13). Pillar 3 ESG templates not completed or limited.
limited_disclosure_metrics.recommendation: Expand disclosed metrics to include financed emissions, EU Taxonomy Green Asset Ratio, sectoral exposure breakdowns, and physical risk concentration analysis.

partial_credit_integration.title: Partial Climate Risk Integration in Credit Process
partial_credit_integration.description: Climate risks considered only for selected sectors or only at origination, not throughout the full credit lifecycle (E8 partial).
partial_credit_integration.recommendation: Extend climate risk assessment to all material sectors and all stages of the credit process (origination, monitoring, review). Develop counterparty-level climate risk scores.

no_liquidity_climate_assessment.title: No Assessment of Climate Impacts on Liquidity
no_liquidity_climate_assessment.description: Institution has not assessed whether climate-related risks could cause liquidity stress (E11). Potential transmission channels through asset devaluation and funding access not analysed.
no_liquidity_climate_assessment.recommendation: Assess climate risk transmission channels to liquidity. Incorporate climate scenarios in ILAAP and liquidity stress testing where material.
